$comment: VENDORED COPY for the WS-A contract test in claude-copilot/tools/cc. Source of truth is copilot-control-tower/docs/01-architecture/schemas/doctor.schema.json — update this copy from there, not the reverse.
title: copilot doctor --json
description: Health verdict Control Tower renders in the tray. Mirrors `cc memory check`. Exit 0 clean / 1 any fail / 2 env error. `status` is computed CLI-side; the app only renders it. See the `status` property below for the authoritative ~10-state enum (corrected 2026-07-07 from an earlier draft `healthy|degraded|failed`).

host.description: Host identifier the verdict was computed for.

generated_at.description: When this verdict was produced. Present in the upstream design's `doctor --json` example (design-control-tower-integration.md §1.1); not marked required since cli-contract.md's prose table omits it, but the CLI is expected to emit it.

status.description: CLI-computed overall status (per-host, worst-wins across hosts). This is the authoritative ~10-state machine from the upstream design (05-control-tower.md §2 table, lines ~35-48), reconciled against this repo's docs/product-design/04-experience-design/50-ux-design.md status-state matrix. Precedence (worst -> best): it-config-incomplete > signed-out > needs-attention > offline > syncing > update-available > healthy; setup-needed/waiting-for-network/updating-app are lifecycle states outside that ladder (first-run / self-update). This field is DISTINCT from the per-checker `severity` (pass|warn|fail) below -- do not conflate the two.
status.$comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-07: replaces an earlier draft enum `healthy|degraded|failed`, which was wrong on two counts -- (1) it conflated the top-level `status` with per-checker `severity` (pass|warn|fail), and (2) it did not match the upstream design at all. Upstream source of truth: claude-copilot/docs/80-initiatives/01-ecosystem-extensions/05-control-tower.md §2 (the state table) and .../research/design-control-tower-integration.md §2.1 (the 6-state UI machine directly sourced from `doctor.status`/`freshness`/`update`: healthy, syncing, update-available, needs-attention, signed-out, offline). The remaining 4 values (setup-needed, it-config-incomplete, waiting-for-network, updating-app) are Control-Tower/app-lifecycle states that 05-control-tower.md §2 folds into the same single worst-wins status; architecture explicitly says 'status is computed by the CLI, not Control Tower' for the whole set, so they are encoded together here. This repo's ux-design.md 50-ux-design.md status matrix additionally lists an 11th row, 'CLI-unreadable / Error' (exit 2, schema out of range, or a missing security field) -- that is intentionally NOT a value in this enum: by definition the CLI failed to produce a parseable doctor verdict in that case, so it is Control Tower's OWN client-side fallback state, never a value the CLI emits inside a valid `doctor.json` instance. Confirm this split with the CLI owner at WS-A freeze; see cli-contract.md 'Freeze status & source of truth'.

offline.description: True when the verdict was produced without network reachability.

items.$comment: The contract lists an explicit trailing ellipsis on the checker object, so it is intentionally open/extensible; additionalProperties is left true. Known security-relevant fields are required. Optional fields below (layer, detail, local_sha, remote_sha, path, escalate) are documented from the upstream design's `doctor --json` example (design-control-tower-integration.md §1.1) but not required, since not every checker emits all of them.

detail.description: Human-readable one-line finding text, e.g. 'org tip a1b2 < remote c3d4'.

repair.description: Repair token the CLI understands, echoed back so Control Tower can offer a one-click Fix via `copilot repair --only <checker.id>`, or null when no repair applies.
repair.$comment: Confirmed against design-control-tower-integration.md §1.1 example (`"repair": "git pull --ff-only"`): a nullable repair-token string, not a boolean 'repair available' flag. This was already correctly encoded in the prior draft; the uncertainty note is now resolved.

destructive.description: Whether the associated repair is destructive. Security-relevant: a missing value is treated as destructive (fail-closed), so the CLI must always emit it.

layer.description: Layer this checker's finding pertains to, when applicable (e.g. 'org', 'dept-finance').

path.description: Materialized-file path this finding pertains to, when applicable.

escalate.description: Who this finding should escalate to when Bob can't act on it alone, e.g. 'user'.

identity.description: The signed-in identity this credential belongs to, e.g. 'bob@acme'.

scope.description: Layer scope this credential covers, e.g. 'org+dept-finance'.

state.description: Fail-closed credential state. `expired` -> transient, re-auth offered (signed-out, recoverable); `revoked` -> permanent, drives fail-closed deprovision-offer per design-control-tower-integration.md §4. Only failing/needs-attention entries are expected in this array (a live/valid credential need not be listed).

auth.$comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-07: tightened to the shape shown in the upstream design's actual `doctor --json` example (design-control-tower-integration.md §1.1, lines ~44-48): `{identity, scope, state, expires_at}`, with `state` in `expired|revoked` per the escalation-ladder text in the same doc (§2.1 line ~106, §4). NOTE: an earlier pass proposed `{layer, state, days_to_expiry}` for this field -- that shape does NOT belong to `doctor --json`; it is the shape of a *different* schema, the opt-in fleet-telemetry payload's `auth[]` array (design-control-tower-integration.md §6.2, a per-org-telemetry-endpoint event, not a doctor verdict). Flagging this as a newly-found divergence from the prior investigation's note.

allOf.$comment: Encodes the 'a false Healthy is impossible' invariant to the degree it is structurally encodable: a healthy verdict may not be offline, may not contain any checker whose severity is fail, and may not contain any auth entry in a fail-closed state (expired/revoked), since Signed-out outranks Healthy in the precedence ladder. The score-threshold component of the invariant (score >= threshold) cannot be encoded here because the design never fixes the numeric threshold.
